# Methods

This chapter exercises the breadth of identifier types `manubot.cite`
understands (the same resolver `quartobot resolve` calls from the
pre-render hook).

A Crossref DOI: @doi:10.1093/bioinformatics/btv145 — fastQC-style quality
control, cited here purely to demonstrate DOI resolution against a
bioinformatics paper.

A PubMed identifier: @pmid:31477126 — cited via PMID, manubot resolves
via NCBI's E-utilities and falls back to Crossref for any DOI it
recovers.

An arXiv preprint: @arxiv:2104.10729 — preprint with no formal Crossref
entry; manubot resolves the metadata from the arXiv API.

A book by ISBN: @isbn:9780262035613 — manubot routes ISBN lookups
through a chain of open bibliographic sources.

And `@quarto2024` again, hand-curated. Notice that the same Quarto
reference can appear in multiple chapters without duplicating in the
final bibliography — that's standard pandoc citeproc behavior, but
worth confirming for the book pattern.

All four persistent-identifier citations above resolved without anyone
typing a bibliography entry. The author wrote four short strings; the
pre-render hook did the rest.
